# Contributing to Fable Frog Feedback

Thank you for helping improve Fable Frog! This guide explains how to participate effectively in this issue tracker.

## Before You Post

1. **Search existing issues** — your bug or idea may already be tracked. If it is, add a 👍 reaction instead of opening a duplicate.
2. **Check Discussions** — for questions, setup help, or rough ideas, use [Discussions](https://github.com/Fable-Frog/fablefrog-feedback/discussions) instead of Issues.
3. **Server vs. app** — if the problem is with your Audiobookshelf server (not the Fable Frog iOS app), file it at [audiobookshelf/audiobookshelf](https://github.com/audiobookshelf/audiobookshelf/issues).

## Filing a Good Issue

Use the [issue templates](https://github.com/Fable-Frog/fablefrog-feedback/issues/new/choose) — they'll guide you through providing the right information.

Tips for high-quality reports:

- **Be specific** — "playback stops after 5 minutes on Wi-Fi" is more actionable than "playback doesn't work."
- **Include versions** — app version, iOS version, and server version help narrow things down.
- **Add screenshots or recordings** — a 10-second screen recording is worth a thousand words.
- **One issue per report** — if you have multiple bugs, file them separately so each can be tracked independently.

## How Triage Works

### Community Reactions

Your 👍 reactions directly influence what gets prioritized. Instead of commenting "+1", use the reaction button on the issue — it keeps threads clean and gives us accurate counts.

**→ [See most-upvoted issues](https://github.com/Fable-Frog/fablefrog-feedback/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Areactions-%2B1-desc)**

### Maintainer Workflow

1. New issues land with the `needs-triage` label.
2. A maintainer reviews and applies:
   - **Status emoji** on the issue: 👀 (reviewing), ✅ (confirmed), 🚀 (planned), 🎉 (shipped)
   - **Labels** for type, priority, and area
3. Issues with 10+ 👍 reactions automatically receive the `popular` label.

### If We Need More Information

If an issue is labeled `needs-info`, we need your help to investigate further. Please respond within 14 days — after that, the issue will be automatically closed. You can always reopen it later with the requested details.

## Stale Issues

Issues with no activity for 60 days are marked `stale` and closed after 14 more days. This keeps the tracker focused on active problems. Exemptions: anything labeled `priority/critical`, `priority/high`, `planned`, or `in-progress`.

## Code of Conduct

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). Please be respectful and constructive in all interactions.
